ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 covers fish finger as a food product category. The specific Old Chang Kee cheezy variant with cheese filling on a stick is not individually represented; closest_parent applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one stick containing 3 fish finger pieces, weighing approximately 90 g, as sold by Old Chang Kee.

nutrient_source.title: Fish finger, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15209 / analogous breaded fried fish product); adapted with cheese-filling adjustment based on USDA Branded Food data for processed cheese contribution
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for Old Chang Kee Cheezy Fish Finger On A Stick. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded fried fish products (fish sticks/fingers, cooked) and adjusted upward for protein, fat, and sodium to account for the processed cheese filling component. Energy, macros, and sodium reflect a composite estimate consistent with similar commercial breaded cheese-fish snack products. Values are presented per 100 g of the ready-to-eat product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on breaded fried fish products and refined wheat breadcrumb coating
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Old Chang Kee Cheezy Fish Finger On A Stick. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried fish finger products. The breadcrumb wheat coating contributes moderate glycemic carbohydrates, but the protein and fat content from fish and cheese substantially blunt the glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database lists fish and protein-rich breaded products in the low-GI range (GI 35–45). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 18.5) / 100 ≈ 7.0, based on 90 g serving containing approximately 18.5 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg per 100 g; approximately 432 mg per 90 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this snack due to its high sodium content from the breaded coating, cheese filling, and seasoning, which can contribute significantly to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from wheat breadcrumb coating, partially offset by low GI (estimated 38) and high protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this snack in moderation; the low estimated GI and high protein-fat content from fish and cheese help moderate postprandial glucose response, but portion control is advised given the refined carbohydrate coating and caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese filling (3.9 g per 100 g) and deep-frying oil, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this deep-fried, cheese-filled snack due to its saturated fat and sodium content, both of which are risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in excess.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheezy Fish Finger On A Stick is a popular Singaporean snack sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known local chain specialising in curry puffs and fried snacks. The product consists of three pieces of breaded, deep-fried white fish fingers filled with processed cheese, skewered together on a single stick for convenient on-the-go eating. The exterior is golden and crispy from the wheat breadcrumb coating, while the interior is moist and flaky fish with a creamy, savoury cheese centre. The snack is sold at approximately SGD 2.40 per stick. Nutritionally, it provides moderate energy (approximately 235 kcal per 100 g) with a balanced macronutrient profile: protein from the fish (12.5 g per 100 g), fat from the cheese and frying oil (11.8 g per 100 g), and carbohydrates from the breadcrumb coating (20.5 g per 100 g). The sodium content is high at 480 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The estimated glycemic index is low (approximately 38), as the protein and fat content blunt the glycemic response from the wheat coating. The cheese filling contributes calcium and vitamin B12. Clinically, this snack is moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI but refined carbohydrate coating, and moderate-to-high concern for cardiovascular disease and hypertension due to saturated fat and sodium. It is classified as a processed fish snack and is halal-certified under Old Chang Kee's operations. It is not suitable for those with fish, gluten, egg, or dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Food entry exists for Old Chang Kee Cheezy Fish Finger On A Stick. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded fried fish products with a composite adjustment for the cheese filling. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for breaded fish products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is systematic and the analogous source is approved.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy breaded fried fish product data, adjusted for the cheese filling component. An exact product-level entry from HPB Singapore Foodo was not found for this specific Old Chang Kee product. 2. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; breaded fried fish products contain negligible vitamin C, confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central reference data for this food category. 4. trans_fat_g of 0.1 reflects trace levels from frying oil; this is a trace value consistent with commercial frying operations. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 4.2 g from frying oil composition), polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 2.8 g. 6. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS Singapore. 7. Serving size of 90 g (3 pieces per stick) is based on the product description stating 3 pieces per stick, with each piece estimated at approximately 30 g based on comparable commercial fish finger products. 8. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source for this food category and not estimable without product-specific laboratory data.
